title: Dividing negative numbers
description: Practice dividing integers (single-digit divisors and quotients).

hints: $0$ divided by any other number is $0$.
How many groups of $-9$ do we take away from $63$ to equal $0$?

We can *add* $7$ groups of $-9$ to $63$ to equal $0$.

So we can *take away* $-7$ groups of $-9$.

Pattern alert: A positive divided by a negative is always a negative.
How many groups of $-8$ do we take away from $48$ to equal $0$?

We can *add* $6$ groups of $-8$ to $48$ to equal $0$.

So we can *take away* $-6$ groups of $-8$.

Pattern alert: A positive divided by a negative is always a negative.
How many groups of $-9$ do we take away from $27$ to equal $0$?

We can *add* $3$ groups of $-9$ to $27$ to equal $0$.

So we can *take away* $-3$ groups of $-9$.

Pattern alert: A positive divided by a negative is always a negative.
How many groups of $-6$ do we take away from $12$ to equal $0$?

We can *add* $2$ groups of $-6$ to $12$ to equal $0$.

So we can *take away* $-2$ groups of $-6$.

Pattern alert: A positive divided by a negative is always a negative.
How many groups of $-1$ do we take away from $6$ to equal $0$?

We can *add* $6$ groups of $-1$ to $6$ to equal $0$.

So we can *take away* $-6$ groups of $-1$.

Pattern alert: A positive divided by a negative is always a negative.
How many groups of $-2$ do we take away from $2$ to equal $0$?

We can *add* $1$ group of $-2$ to $2$ to equal $0$.

So we can *take away* $-1$ group of $-2$.

Pattern alert: A positive divided by a negative is always a negative.
Suppose we split $24$ *negative* integer chips into $6$ equal groups. How much is in each group?

[[☃ image 1]]

Each group represents $-4$.

Pattern alert: A negative divided by a positive is a negative.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each group represents $-7$.

Pattern alert: A negative divided by a positive is a negative.

Pattern alert: A negative divided by a positive is a negative.
